id.description: Stable slug; kebab-case; unique across all meshes

category.description: One of: proxy, search, ocr, geo, nlp, content, domains, security, recon, infrastructure, web_analysis, visualization, registry, dashboard

kind.description: What kind of deployable this is. Orthogonal to mesh. 'container' = runs as a Docker service on the dockerhost (has host_port, container_port, /health, Dockerfile, deploy.yaml, LXC workspace). 'static' = static GitHub Pages site (no port, no container, no workspace, no /health). fleet-runner gates kind-specific operations on this field — kind=static is skipped by health/smoke/deploy/clone-missing/audit-port/bump-version/update-dep.

language.description: Primary implementation language. For kind=container this drives bulk-operation filters (e.g. 'update-dep' only touches go services). For kind=static it is informational. Default for legacy 0exec/0crawl entries is 'go'; default for pages is 'html'.

url.description: Public base URL of the service, no trailing slash

example_path.description: Path + query that produces a real response; begins with '/'. Example: '/?url=https://example.com'. May be empty if the service has no canonical example.

path_template.description: auth=path_token: how the token is embedded in the path, with '{token}' placeholder. Example: '/t/{token}'

public_demo_token.description: auth=path_token: an intentionally-public demo token consumers may default to. NEVER include real secrets here.

auth_help.description: Canonical short label for what auth a caller needs. Computed by generate.py from the auth object. UIs render verbatim — do NOT re-derive in JS/templates (that's what produced the historical 'No auth' bug on api_key services).

pages_url.description: kind=static only. Canonical GitHub Pages URL. For kind=container this field MUST NOT be set.

pages_source_branch.description: kind=static only. Branch the Pages build is served from (typically 'main' or 'gh-pages'). Informational for the catalog UI.

port.description: DEPRECATED — ambiguous (host vs container). Use host_port / container_port instead.

host_port.description: Port bound on the dockerhost. This is what the gateway nginx proxy_pass points at. Single source of truth for IaC rendering. See private fleet-state/OPS.md for the dockerhost address.

container_port.description: Port the service listens on INSIDE the container. Informational; not used by gateway routing.

trl.description: Technology Readiness Level achieved. 1-3 toy, 4-5 developing, 6-7 real, 8-9 production. Optional; absence means not yet assessed.

trl_evidence.description: One-line justification for the TRL number (what concretely makes it that level).

trl_ceiling.description: Maximum TRL realistically achievable with CPU-only smart logic + free public data. Null means no known ceiling. Repos with low ceilings are deprecation candidates.

trl_ceiling_reason.description: One-line reason for the ceiling (e.g. 'needs libpostal trained data', 'requires DOM canary injection', 'wraps paid threat intel feeds').

trl_assessed_at.description: ISO date the TRL was last evaluated. Re-assess if older than ~90 days.

trl_assessor.description: Who/what assessed: 'claude-session-YYYY-MM-DD' for AI-assisted, 'human' for manual review.
